title: The Burning of Ashveil
description: Ashveil — then a major city larger than Vareth — was destroyed in a single night. No army claimed responsibility. No siege was recorded. By dawn, only ash and silence. The cause remains unknown. The Wanderer was reportedly seen walking away from the smoke.
consequences: Destruction of a major population center
Created the current political vacuum in the Free Coast region

title: The Fall of the Grey Keep
description: The Grey Keep's garrison stopped responding to communications. A relief party found it empty — no bodies, no blood, no signs of struggle. The doors were locked from the inside. Aran Voss ordered it quietly reoccupied and the incident not discussed.
consequences: Grey Keep lost as a reliable outpost
Added to local folklore about cursed places

title: Charter of the Compact
description: Five trading houses in Solmark signed the Charter of the Compact, creating a mutual defense and trade agreement. The Compact has no king — only the council and the ledger.
consequences: Created the Compact as a political entity
Established Solmark as a free city
Began the rivalry with Northern Reach

title: Founding of Vareth
description: The first permanent fortification on the Var River. Originally a watchtower controlling the northern passage through the Ashspine foothills. Grew into a city within two generations.
consequences: Established Northern Reach control over northern passage

title: The Siege of Vareth
description: The Compact's allied forces besieged Vareth for forty days. Aran Voss, twenty-three years old, had taken command of the city after his father's death on the third day. The siege was broken not by military force but by a flood that cut the supply lines. Both sides called it luck.
consequences: Aran Voss established as ruler of Vareth
Northern Reach survived as a political entity
The Compact retreated but was not defeated

title: The Treaty of Solmark
description: A temporary ceasefire negotiated through the merchant networks, not through official ambassadors. Mira Solhand is credited with arranging the initial meetings. Both sides signed and immediately began preparing to break it.
consequences: Temporary halt to active military conflict
Established secret trade routes between factions
Mira Solhand gained significant political influence
